content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, you’ll extend your application’s data blueprint by defining the `Post` and `Comment` models. These models, along with the previously defined `User` model, allow us to represent blog-like content and discussions in the application. Flask-SQLAlchemy translates these models into relational database tables.

---

### 🎯 Goal

Define the structure for `Post` and `Comment` models in a Python file (e.g., `models.py`), specifying columns and relationships to represent how posts and comments interact with users and each other.

---

### 🔑 Key Concepts

* **`db.Model`**: The base class that all SQLAlchemy models inherit from.
* **`db.Column`**: Declares a column in the database table, specifying its data type and constraints.
* **`db.ForeignKey`**: Creates a relationship between a column in one table and the primary key of another table.
* **`db.relationship`**: Allows you to access related objects easily in Python (e.g., all comments on a post).


* **Relationships Across Models**:

  * A **User** → has many → **Posts**
  * A **User** → has many → **Comments**
  * A **Post** → belongs to → **User**
  * A **Post** → has many → **Comments**
  * A **Comment** → belongs to both → **User** and **Post**


---

### 📝 Define the Post Model

#### **Post Model Structure Summary:**

| Attribute     | Type              | Constraints/Details                       | Purpose                                        |
| ------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `id`          | `db.Integer`      | Primary Key                               | Unique identifier for each post.               |
| `title`       | `db.String(120)`  | Not Nullable                              | Title of the post.                             |
| `content`     | `db.Text`         | Not Nullable                              | Main content of the post.                      |
| `date_posted` | `db.DateTime`     | Not Nullable, UTC timezone aware, Indexed | When the post was created.                     |
| `author_id`   | `db.Integer`      | Foreign key to `User.id`                  | ID of the user who wrote the post.             |
| `author`      | `db.relationship` | back\_populates=`posts`                   | Reference to the `User` who authored the post. |
| `comments`    | `db.relationship` | back\_populates=`post`, cascade delete    | Access to comments on this post.               |

---

### 💬 Define the Comment Model

#### **Comment Model Structure Summary:**

| Attribute     | Type              | Constraints/Details                       | Purpose                              |
| ------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `id`          | `db.Integer`      | Primary Key                               | Unique identifier for each comment.  |
| `content`     | `db.Text`         | Not Nullable                              | The actual comment content.          |
| `date_posted` | `db.DateTime`     | Not Nullable, UTC timezone aware, Indexed | When the comment was made.           |
| `post_id`     | `db.Integer`      | Foreign key to `Post.id`                  | ID of the post this comment is on.   |
| `user_id`     | `db.Integer`      | Foreign key to `User.id`                  | ID of the user who made the comment. |
| `post`        | `db.relationship` | back\_populates=`comments`                | Reference to the related post.       |
| `user`        | `db.relationship` | back\_populates=`comments`                | Reference to the user who commented. |
